content_markdown: Which of the following is the internal energy of a system?

options.label: A
options.text: The total thermal energy gained by the system during melting and boiling.

options.label: B
options.text: The sum of the potential and the kinetic energies of the particles of the system.

options.label: C
options.text: The total external work done on the system during melting and boiling.

options.label: D
options.text: The change in the potential energy of the system that occurs during melting and boiling.

vibe_explanation: Internal energy is defined as the total microscopic energy of a system. This consists of the sum of the random kinetic energy of the particles (associated with temperature) and the intermolecular potential energy (associated with the phase/state of the substance).
